☀️ Vegas in July
Expect 100°F+ daily. The Adventuredome (fully indoor AC) and Mandalay Beach pool are your best friends. Sunscreen every morning. Hydrate constantly. The Strip is walkable at night — not at noon.

Full Trip Agenda
Day by Day
July 3–8, 2026 · Mandalay Bay, Las Vegas
Day 0 · Thursday, July 2
Early Arrival
South Strip — Mandalay Bay
▼
Afternoon
Quincy + Staci Check In
Settle into Mandalay Bay. Standard check-in is 3pm.
Evening
Dinner — Border Grill or Libertine Social
In-property, low-key. Casual, no reservation needed.
Late
Walk the South Strip
Luxor pyramid, NYNY rollercoaster lights, Vegas at night vibes. First-night reset, no agenda.
Day 1 · Friday, July 3
Family Arrives
South Strip — Mandalay Bay + Vegas Sign
▼
7:00 AM
Quincy + Staci: Easy Breakfast
In-room or grab coffee. Get ready to greet arrivals. Stephanie's family lands ~8am.
8:00 AM
Stephanie + Carlton + Lelaini + Levi Land at LAS
Pre-booked limo waiting at arrivals.
BOOK AHEAD
8:30 AM
Limo Transfer LAS → Mandalay Bay
20 min ride. Kids get the Vegas Strip arrival moment.
9:30 AM
Group Breakfast — Citizens Kitchen or Café Mandalay
Catch-up time for the adults. Kids settle in.
12:00 PM
Marques + Zayden + Zoey Arrive
Full crew united at Mandalay Bay.
12:30 PM
Welcome Lunch — Border Grill or Slice of Vegas
No travel needed. Everyone just landed. Mexican or pizza, kid-friendly.
2:00 PM
Welcome to Las Vegas Sign — Group Photo
5 min drive. Free parking lot. Expect 10–20 min wait for the official sign side.
FREE
3:00 PM
Pool / Mandalay Beach
Kids burn off travel energy. Lazy river + wave pool. Free for hotel guests.
FREEKIDS
6:30 PM
Group Dinner — Citizens Kitchen, Slice of Vegas, or House of Blues
In-property. No reservation pressure.
8:00 PM
Wind Down
Kids to bed early. Adults can hit the casino floor briefly. Tomorrow is a big day.
Day 2 · Saturday, July 4
Independence Day
North Strip → Sphere → Fireworks
▼
8:00 AM
Breakfast — Peppermill Diner
2985 Las Vegas Blvd S. Iconic vintage Vegas diner. Arrive by 8 to beat the wait. Walk-in only.
ARRIVE EARLY
10:00 AM
Drive to Circus Circus
2880 Las Vegas Blvd S. Free self-parking. Adventuredome opens at 11am.
FREE PARKING
11:00 AM
🎢 ADVENTUREDOME — Indoor Amusement Park
Coasters, mini golf, bumper cars, midway games, free clown show at noon and 1pm. AC + indoor = perfect midday escape from 100°F+ heat.
KIDS48" MIN FOR COASTERS
1:30 PM
Lunch — Inside Adventuredome or Food Court
Keep it light — Sphere is the main event.
2:30 PM
Drive Back to Mandalay Bay — AC Reset
Change clothes, sunscreen check. Brief downtime before the show.
4:00 PM
Drive to Sphere
255 Sands Ave. Allow 30 min in July 4 traffic. Park in Sphere garage (~$30–50). Doors open 60–90 min before show.
BOOK AHEAD
5:00 PM
🌐 WIZARD OF OZ AT SPHERE
75 minute runtime. Aim for 5pm or 6pm show. Verify your booked time. The world's largest immersive venue.
BOOKED
6:30 PM
Black Tap CrazyShakes — Venetian
Grand Canal Shoppes. Kids get CrazyShakes (~$18–22). Adults get boozy milkshakes. Milkshakes only — save appetite for dinner.
7:15 PM
Group Dinner — Buddy V's or Grand Lux Café
Venetian. Walk-up or OpenTable. Gondoliers entertain the kids while you wait.
9:00 PM
🎆 FIREWORKS — From the Booked Room
Typically 9pm sharp. ~15–20 min show. Back at Mandalay Bay.
9:30 PM
Adults-Only Night #1 Begins
Ramsay's Kitchen + Late Strip. Stephanie + Carlton take sitter duty.
ADULTS ONLY
Adults Night #1
10pm — Late-seating dinner at Ramsay's Kitchen, Harrah's. Avg $80–120/person. Midnight — Walk the late Strip: Bellagio fountains, Linq Promenade, or cocktail at Vesper Bar (Cosmopolitan).
Day 3 · Sunday, July 5
Recovery & Pool
South Strip — Mandalay Bay Pool Day
▼
9:00 AM
Slow Breakfast — In-Property or Room Service
Hangover-proof for the adults.
10:30 AM
🌊 POOL DAY — Mandalay Beach
Wave pool, lazy river, sand beach. Get there by 10:30 to grab grouped chairs.
FREE GUESTSKIDS
12:30 PM
Pool Lunch — Beach Bar & Grill
Burgers, dogs, smoothies — kid-easy. Right inside the pool complex.
3:00 PM
Continue Pool or AC Break
Sun is brutal 12–4pm. Rotate kids to room for AC break if needed.
7:00 PM
Group Dinner — Slice of Vegas or Citizens Kitchen
In-property. No reservation pressure.
8:30 PM
Optional: Shark Reef Aquarium
Open till 10pm. Kids love it. $31 adult / $25 kid. Great after a pool day.
KIDS LOVE IT
Day 4 · Monday, July 6
Big Show Day
Area15 (Omega Mart) + MJ ONE Cirque
▼
9:00 AM
Easy In-Room or In-Property Breakfast
Save appetite for later.
11:00 AM
Drive to Area15
3215 S Rancho Dr. 15 min drive. Free parking (small fee for non-locals). Opens at 11am Mon.
11:30 AM
🌀 OMEGA MART by Meow Wolf
Immersive art experience. No time limit. Plan 1.5–2.5 hours. Under 18 must be with adult.
BOOK AHEADKIDS WITH ADULT
2:00 PM
Lunch at Area15
Five Iron, Beast (Todd English), or food hall. Kid-friendly options across the board.
3:00 PM
Optional Add-Ons at Area15
Lift-Off ride, Game Depot, dueling axes (adults), VR. À la carte $6–69 per attraction.
4:30 PM
Drive Back to Mandalay Bay — Pool/AC Reset
Naps for the little kids.
6:00 PM
Quick Group Dinner — Keep It Light
Show is at 7. Try Libertine Social or in-room.
6:45 PM
Walk to MJ ONE Theatre
In-property at Mandalay Bay. Doors open 30 min before.
7:00 PM
🎭 MICHAEL JACKSON ONE — CIRQUE DU SOLEIL
Mandalay Bay. 90 min runtime. Ages 5+ only. Walk from the lobby.
BOOKEDAGES 5+
9:30 PM
Adults-Only Night #2 Begins
Strip cocktail crawl. Marques takes sitter duty.
ADULTS ONLY
Adults Night #2 — Cocktail Crawl
Stop 1: Vesper Bar (Cosmopolitan) — refined, $20 cocktails. Stop 2: The Barbershop Cuts & Cocktails (Cosmopolitan) — speakeasy through a working barbershop, live music after 10. Stop 3: Skyfall Lounge (Delano/Mandalay Bay) — 64th floor views, stumble distance to your room.
Day 5 · Tuesday, July 7
Last Chill Day
South Strip — Mandalay Bay + Misc.
▼
8:00 AM
Easy In-Property Breakfast
No rush, no agenda. Citizens Kitchen, Café Mandalay, or in-room.
9:30 AM
Quincy + Staci Pack and Prep for Departure
Bell desk holds bags after checkout.
10:00 AM
🌊 POOL TIME — Mandalay Beach
Lazy river, wave pool, sand beach. Get there by 10 to grab grouped chairs.
FREE GUESTS
12:00 PM
Group Farewell Lunch at the Pool
Beach Bar & Grill OR Slice of Vegas. Casual, kid-friendly, no reservation needed.
1:00 PM
Quincy + Staci Depart Vegas
Goodbyes. Sedan loaded. Home to Peoria or to LAS if flying.
3:30 PM
Optional: Shark Reef Aquarium
In-property at Mandalay Bay. $31 adult / $25 kid. Great cooldown activity.
KIDS
7:00 PM
Casual Group Dinner
Citizens Kitchen, Slice of Vegas, or Border Grill. Walk-in or day-of OpenTable.
8:30 PM
Wind Down — Strip Walk or Last Casino Visit
Kids back to room. Adults: final night in Vegas. Set the room for tomorrow's bag prep.
Day 6 · Wednesday, July 8
Departures
Wrap-Up — Checkout & Head Home
▼
Morning
Remaining Family Check-Outs Begin
Standard checkout 11am. Quincy + Staci already departed Tuesday.
Morning
Final Kid Breakfast — Café In-Property
Easy, no agenda.
By 11 AM
All Check-Outs Complete
Bell desk holds bags free if flights are later.
Afternoon
Departures
Marques + kids in the Expedition. Stephanie's family via rideshare XL or limo. 20 min from Mandalay to LAS.
Hype Brochure
Zayden's Vegas
July 3–8, 2026 · The Adventure Edition
🏙️ Cover
🎢 Adventure
🎭 Shows
🍕 Food
🌊 Pool
🗺️ Explore
💌 Love Note
⭐ Closing
Las Vegas · July 2026
ZAYDEN
Las Vegas, Nevada
July 3 — 8, 2026
◆ ◇ ◆
"The city is loud. You're louder. This one's yours."
"Five days. Zero chill. Maximum everything. Vegas was built for kids like you."

You've heard about it. You've seen it in movies. Now it's real — and it's yours. Five days in Las Vegas with the whole crew, and every single day is packed with something that's going to make your jaw drop.

We're talking an indoor amusement park where the AC is cranked and the coasters are waiting. The Sphere — a building so big it has its own weather. Omega Mart, a place that looks like a grocery store but is actually a portal to another dimension. And a wave pool, a lazy river, and a sand beach — all inside the hotel.

🎢 Adventure HQ
RIDE EVERYTHING. FEAR NOTHING.
"An entire amusement park. Indoors. In Vegas. On the Fourth of July."

Independence Day. The whole country is celebrating. And you? You're inside the Adventuredome at Circus Circus — five acres of indoor amusement park where the temperature is always perfect and the rides never stop.

Coasters. Bumper cars. Mini golf. Midway games. And at noon and 1pm — a free clown show right in the middle of it all. After the Adventuredome, the Sphere is next.

Adventuredome
🎢 Rides & Thrills
Five-acre indoor theme park at Circus Circus. Roller coasters, bumper cars, mini golf, midway games — all under one giant pink dome.
📅 July 4 · 11am–1:30pm · Day pass ~$60 adult / $30 junior · Free clown show noon & 1pm
The Sphere
🌐 Immersive Experience
The world's largest spherical building showing Wizard of Oz. 160,000 sq ft of LED screen. You don't watch the movie — you're inside it.
📅 July 4 · 5pm · 255 Sands Ave · 75 min runtime · Already booked
🎭 The Show Must Go On
MICHAEL JACKSON. CIRQUE DU SOLEIL. LIVE.
"Ninety minutes of humans doing things that should be physically impossible. To Michael Jackson songs."

You know who Michael Jackson is. You've heard the songs. But you have never seen anything like MJ ONE by Cirque du Soleil. 90 minutes of the most insane acrobatics, aerial stunts, and live performance you will ever witness. Performers fly through the air. The stage transforms. The music hits different when you're watching people defy gravity to it.

Best part? It's right inside your hotel at Mandalay Bay. Walk from the lobby to the theatre. No cars, no traffic.

MJ ONE — Cirque du Soleil
🎭 World-Class Show
Michael Jackson's music brought to life by the world's greatest circus performers. Aerial acrobatics, breakdancing, and a stage that transforms every 10 minutes.
📅 July 6 · 7pm · Mandalay Bay Theatre · 90 min · Ages 5+ · Walk from lobby
🍕 The Foodie Mission
EAT LIKE THE CITY NEVER CLOSES
"A milkshake with a whole slice of cake on top. In Las Vegas. On the Fourth of July."

Vegas doesn't just do big shows and big rides. It does big food. On July 4th, after the Sphere, the crew rolls to the Venetian for Black Tap CrazyShakes — milkshakes so loaded with toppings they barely fit through the door. Cotton candy. Cake slices. Sprinkles on everything.

Black Tap CrazyShakes
🥤 Legendary Dessert
The most over-the-top milkshakes on the planet. Topped with cotton candy, cake slices, and more candy. You'll need both hands.
📅 July 4 · Post-Sphere · Venetian Grand Canal Shoppes · ~$18–22/shake
Peppermill Diner
🥞 Vegas Institution
Open since 1972. Neon-lit booths, massive portions, and the kind of breakfast that powers a full day of rides and shows.
📅 July 4 · 8am · 2985 Las Vegas Blvd S · Walk-in only · Arrive early
🌊 Splash Zone
YOUR HOTEL HAS A BEACH
"A wave pool. In the desert. In a hotel. This is Las Vegas doing Las Vegas things."

Most hotels have a pool. Mandalay Bay has a beach. Real sand. A wave pool that generates actual waves. A lazy river that loops around the whole complex. All free for hotel guests.

The wave pool hits every 90 seconds. Pool days: July 3, 5, and 7. Get there by 10am to grab chairs.

Mandalay Beach
🌊 Wave Pool + Lazy River
11-acre pool complex with wave pool, lazy river, sand beach, and multiple pool areas. Free for hotel guests. Best pool setup in Las Vegas.
📅 July 3, 5, 7 · 9am–7pm · Free for guests · Get there by 10am
Shark Reef Aquarium
🦈 Underwater World
2,000+ animals including sharks, rays, sea turtles, and jellyfish. Walk through a glass tunnel with sharks swimming over your head.
📅 Optional · July 5 or 7 · $31 adult / $25 kid · Open till 10pm
🗺️ Explore Mode
THE GROCERY STORE THAT BROKE REALITY
"It's a grocery store. Except it's a portal to another dimension. Just go."

It looks like a grocery store. It has shelves. It has products. And then you walk through a door in the back and reality just stops. Omega Mart by Meow Wolf is an immersive art experience unlike anything that exists anywhere else in the world. Secret rooms. Hidden passages. Entire worlds built inside what looks like a supermarket.

Omega Mart
🌀 Immersive Art World
Meow Wolf's Las Vegas installation. A fake grocery store that's actually a labyrinth of secret rooms, surreal art, and hidden worlds. No time limit.
📅 July 6 · 11:30am · Area15 · $59 adult / $45 kid · 1.5–2.5 hrs
Welcome to Las Vegas Sign
📸 Iconic Moment
The most photographed sign in Nevada. Free parking. The whole crew gets the classic Vegas arrival photo. This one goes in the frame.
📅 July 3 · 2pm · S Las Vegas Blvd · Free · 10–20 min wait
A Note
Dear Zayden,

I want you to know that this trip wasn't just planned — it was built for you. Every ride, every show, every milkshake with way too many toppings — I thought about what would make your eyes go wide and your voice go loud, and then I put all of it in one week.

You are the kind of kid who makes every room better just by being in it. Your energy, your laugh, the way you go all-in on everything — Vegas is going to match you. This city was made for people who show up big. And that's exactly what you do.

Ride the coasters. Explore Omega Mart like you're on a mission. Watch the Cirque performers fly and know that one day, people are going to watch you do something that incredible. Eat the milkshake. Get in the wave pool. Stay up a little too late.

This is our family doing what our family does — showing up together, going big together, and making memories that we'll be talking about for the rest of our lives. I love you more than Las Vegas has neon lights. And that's a lot of neon.

— Dad (Marques)

P.S. from Zoey: "Zayden you better not hog the lazy river." 🌊

P.S. from the whole crew: We love you, Z. This one's for you. ✨

P.S. from Quincy + Staci: Can't wait to watch you experience this city for the first time. 🎰

"You came to Vegas. You rode the coasters. You watched humans fly. You walked through a portal in a grocery store. You owned the wave pool. You were here."
Las Vegas, Nevada · July 3–8, 2026
Quincy · Staci · Marques · Zayden · Zoey · Stephanie · Carlton · Lelaini · Levi
Zayden's Vegas. Nobody does it bigger.
